metadata.title:
MAY


PAY BUT CITY
HAS To

pages.raw_text:
The board Of public works is 60
ing ahead with the new Howard
street bridges and paying for the
work with city money.


This Is done on the presumption
that the Washington Water Power
company will reimburse the city
in full when the work is com
pleted.


But will It?


The board has no form of con.
tract or written agreement to COM
pei such payment. The board is
paying for the work and taking
chances on corporation collec-
ton. II the board is as reluctant
in this collection as Chairman Roy
was in assessing the cost to the
company when instructed to do so
by the council the account is likely
to run some time.


A bill of $500 for lumber was
paid by the city yesterday, being
the first expense of the temporary
bridge being erected on Howard
street across the river in place of
the collapsed middle span.


With regard to this payment ].
T. ONO, member of the board of
public works, said:


THE work is going along under
the supervision of the board, and
we will pay all bills until the work
is completed. Then a bill totalling
the whole amount of expense will
be paid into the city treasury by
the Washington Water Power CON-
pany."


Mr.. ONO said he did not believe
the structure could be completed
wl.-.n .Ae estimated expense of
$1,000, although it would not cost
much more.


1t is claimed the bridge will be
ready for streetcar and foot traffic
by the middle of next week.
